title: Dischidia major
taxonomy: Dischidia major is classified as a type of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. More specifically, it is a member of the genus Dischidia, which comprises about 60 species of epiphytic plants native to tropical and subtropical regions. Dischidia major is a species of the genus Dischidia that is native to tropical Asia. The plant is often grown as an ornamental due to its attractive, heart-shaped leaves and ability to thrive in a variety of environments. Dischidia major is often confused with other species of Dischidia due to the similarities in its appearance.

intro: Dischidia major, commonly known as the string of nickels or starfish vine, is a type of epiphytic plant native to tropical Asia, characterized by its unique, star-shaped leaves and ability to thrive in a variety of environments.
